name: Citizens State Bank
description_short: Citizens State Bank Wyoming, IA — Community bank in Iowa offering checking, savings, loans, and investment services across multiple locations since 1893.
description_long:
Citizens State Bank is a long-established community bank headquartered in Wyoming, Iowa, with over 130 years of continuous operation at the same location. Originally founded as Citizens Bank in 1893 by Peter S. Jansen, it was chartered as a state bank in 1913 and has since expanded to serve the communities of Wyoming, Olin, Oxford Junction, and surrounding areas. The bank operates multiple branch locations including offices in Wyoming, Olin, Oxford Junction, and Maquoketa (opened 2020), providing residents with local banking access.

Citizens State Bank offers a comprehensive range of traditional banking services including checking and savings accounts, home loans, long-term investment options, and credit products. Customers can manage accounts online through CSB Online Banking, which allows users to view balances, transaction history, statements, and transfer funds between accounts. The bank also provides mobile banking through CSB GoMobile, enabling account access from any mobile device. Additional services include credit card management, check ordering, ATM access, and forms/calculators for financial planning.

The bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community connection and personal service. With a team boasting over 200 years of combined banking experience, Citizens State Bank positions itself as "big enough to serve all your banking needs — and small enough to care about your financial future." The bank highlights its long-term relationships with customers and involvement in local communities, featuring customer spotlights and community-focused narratives on their website. Their longevity at the same Wyoming location since 1893 demonstrates stability and deep community roots.

As a traditional community bank, Citizens State Bank serves customers seeking personalized banking relationships with local decision-making. However, the website provides limited detail regarding specific product features, rates, fees, or competitive offerings. Consumers comparing rates or seeking specialized products may need to contact the bank directly, as the website emphasizes general capabilities rather than detailed product specifications or promotional rates. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking with account management and fund transfers
Mobile banking via CSB GoMobile app
pros: 130+ years of continuous operation at the same location, demonstrating exceptional stability
Multiple branch locations across Iowa (Wyoming, Olin, Oxford Junction, Maquoketa) for convenience
Online banking platform with account viewing, transaction history, and fund transfers
Mobile banking app (CSB GoMobile) for anytime account access
Staff with over 200 years of combined banking experience
Full-service banking including home loans, investments, credit cards, and business services
Drive-up facility in Wyoming and expanded Maquoketa facility (opened 2020)
cons: Website lacks specific information about account types, fee structures, interest rates, or terms
Limited to Iowa communities; not suitable for customers outside service areas
Minimal product comparison or competitive rate information available online
No mention of specialized digital banking features common at larger institutions
Contact required for most detailed product information and rate inquiries
best_for: Iowa residents in Wyoming, Olin, Oxford Junction, Maquoketa, and surrounding communities seeking local banking
Customers prioritizing personal relationships and community connection over digital-only banking
Small business owners in rural Iowa needing traditional business banking services
Long-term customers seeking stable, established banking institution with deep community roots
diagnosis: Citizens State Bank is best for Iowa residents in its service areas (Wyoming, Olin, Oxford Junction, Maquoketa) who value community connection, personal service, and banking with an institution that has deep local roots and relationships. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal information about specific rates, fees, and product details—customers must contact the bank directly for detailed comparisons, and the bank's geographic limitations make it unsuitable for those outside Iowa.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
